steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines l and k are parallel, cut by a transversal. Since l ∥ k, the angle at line l corresponding to 64° also measures 64° (corresponding angles are equal). Angle a° forms a linear pair with that 64° angle at line l.

steps.title: Apply the linear pair theorem
steps.content: Angles in a linear pair are supplementary — they sum to 180°: $$a + 64 = 180$$ $$a = 180 - 64 = 116$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '26': 26 + 64 = 90, not 180. This would only apply if a and 64° were complementary. ✗
  • Choice B — '64': 64 would apply if a and 64° were corresponding or alternate interior angles (equal). But a° is the supplement of the corresponding angle, not the corresponding angle itself. ✗
  • Choice C — '116': 116 + 64 = 180. Linear pair angles are supplementary. ✓
  • Choice D — '154': 154 + 64 = 218 ≠ 180. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: The corresponding angle to 64° also measures 64°, and a° is its linear pair supplement: a = 180 − 64 = 116. That matches choice C — C is correct.
